items.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld, SA

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across the border to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

gallery.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

thumbnail.altText: Mark Butler wearing a dark suit.

items.title: NT records two deaths from rare mosquito-borne disease

children.content: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A female Aedes aegypti mosquito bites someone's skin.

items.title: NT police officer on trial for allegedly attempting to pervert course of justice

children.content: A jury has heard police officer Karol Jarentowski repeatedly attempted to pressure his former partner into dropping aggravated assault charges against him, including at one point threatening her child support.

image.altText: A group of people in suits walking out of the NT Supreme Court.

items.title: Calls for hastily constructed NT evacuation site to be relocated

children.content: Residents in the regional NT town of Batchelor say they have been left in the dark about the future of a pop-up evacuation centre that was built earlier this year without their consultation for flood evacuees.

gallery.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A mattress hangs over a railing with generators in the background.

items.title: 'Thriller' history book about the bark petitions wins $50k literary prize

children.content: The 1963 Näku Dhäruk or Yirrkala bark petitions led to Australia's first land rights law. A new book about them just won two prizes at the NSW Literary Awards.

image.altText: Clare Wright, 57, long blonde hair and glasses, arms folded, smiles brightly at the camera as she stands in a library.

items.title: Some NT cases could halt if landmark legal challenge succeeds

children.content: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

image.altText: A man with a serious expression sits on a long wooden table with legal books behind him.

items.title: Alice Springs' gruelling West Macs Monster ultra-marathon, in pictures

children.content: Over distances of up to 231km, 573 runners took to the Larapinta Trail outside Alice Springs at the weekend to compete in the 2026 West Macs Monster.

Runners move along a dirt track.

items.title: Parent company orders cattle station to 'cease any plans' for bird cull

children.content: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.

image.altText: A little corella looks down from a gum tree

items.title: Meet the women rangers in Central Australia working to protect country

children.content: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A group of Indigenous women sitting on the ground, stripping leaves from twigs.

children.content: A former Brisbane doctor has been charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault following an extensive police investigation.

items.title: ARIA winner TISM causes $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House

image.altText: Seven men in balaclavas and black suits pose at the Opera House balcony overlooking the Harbour Bridge at Sunset

items.title: Undersea volcanic eruption visible from space

children.content: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.

items.title: Rain 'more than double' forecast drenches Gold Coast amid BOM accuracy concerns

children.content: As flash flooding from record-setting rainfall recedes, some south-east Queensland residents feel that Bureau of Meteorology forecasts fell short of what was experienced.

image.altText: A road closed due to flooding sign in front of a road covered by water.

items.title: Two Italian divers' bodies recovered from underwater cave

children.content: Finnish divers recovered the bodies after they were located deep inside an underwater cave in the Maldives.

items.title: Xi to welcome Putin for visit days after hosting Trump

children.content: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

items.title: 'Silence is not consent': Mother raped by massage therapist

children.content: A Queensland massage therapist has been imprisoned for raping a client who says "silence is not consent" and that the "degrading" experience devastated her emotionally, physically and financially.

items.title: Money stress causing tension in your relationship? This might help

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

items.title: Death penalty rates at 40-year high globally, Amnesty International says

children.content: A crackdown in Iran drove a surge in the number of people executed worldwide in 2025, but China remains the "leading executioner," human rights group Amnesty International says.

items.title: How planes shot down decades ago could be central to Trump's plans in Cuba

children.content: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez says Cuba's right to defend itself should not be used by the US as justification for a potential war.

image.altText: A small, white dual-tail plane flying over dark blue ocean water and boats.

items.title: Israeli forces have detained 11 Australians after intercepting Gaza-bound flotilla

children.content: Israeli forces detain 11 Australians after its navy intercepts ships seeking to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza, flotilla organisers say.

image.altText: A woman sitting on a ship holding a sign calling for someone's freedom, with Palestinian flags flying in background.

items.title: Will the war in Iran make Australia use less plastic?

children.content: Could the war in Iran change the way we recycle back home?
Australia mostly relies on importing plastic from overseas.
As well as petrol and diesel, the Strait of Hormuz is a key delivery route for a crucial backbone of plastic production. But, that's now cut off.
It's led to a growing conversation about how Australia might transition to fully recycling plastic packaging onshore.

image.altText: TN_PlasticIran

items.title: WHO 'deeply concerned' over Ebola outbreak after at least 131 people die

children.content: The cash-strapped World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the Democratic Republic of the Congo as hundreds of cases of Ebola are suspected.

items.title: What we know about the deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque

items.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's medication

children.content: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.

articles.title: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in 'pivot to reduce risk' — as it happened

items.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years

children.content: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

image.altText: A large amount of fish are laid out in a trench

items.title: Why Sam Walker is the great unknown of State of Origin I

children.content: Sam Walker has always seemed like a player destined for the highest levels of rugby league, but ahead of his State of Origin debut, it's impossible to know what comes next for the new Queensland halfback.
